---
title: "Statistical Tests as Linear Models"
authors:
  - admin
date: "2019-06-21"
slug: stat-tests-as-linear
summary: "Jonas Kristoffer Lindeløv offers a post on how to teach intro stats using linear models in R."
categories:
  - R
  - Resources
tags:
- linear models
- parametric
- non-parametric
- t-test
- ANOVA
- chi-square
image:
  caption: "SafeHouse Ministries logo."
  focal_point: ""
  preview_only: true
output: blogdown::html_page
---

[Jonas Kristoffer Lindeløv](https://lindeloev.net/) wrote an [excellent tutorial](https://lindeloev.github.io/tests-as-linear/) on how to think of major statistical procedures as extensions of the simple linear model, $y = a \cdot x + b$.

He provides R code for parametric tests and their non-parametric cousins.

Lindeløv produced this helpful graphic:

![Common statistical tests as linear models, by Jonas Kristoffer Lindeløv](/img/linear-tests-cheat-sheet.png)

Check out his main post.
